summary: Iter89 Wallet monitoring closeout: 7/7 backend PASS + frontend admin wallet-overview tab renders flawlessly. All 3 monitoring contracts validated: (1) Rate-limit 5/min/user on POST /api/wallet/send confirmed → HTTP 429 observed after 5 attempts (body mentions '5 per 1 minute'). (2) GET /api/admin/wallet/overview?days=30 returns full shape incl. balances, volumes_by_type, timeseries, top_funded (≤10), anomalies{large_withdrawals, stuck_pending_over_24h, send_spam_last_1h}, engagement_points{total_points, by_source.{wheel,quiz,tap}, total_spins, unique_players}. (3) GET /api/admin/wallet/alerts?limit=20 returns {items:[...]} with kind/alert_key/title/body/created_at/push_sent. (4) withdraw_without_kyc alert triggered (403 KYC_REQUIRED by Bob, $600 TRC20) AND dedup enforced (2nd call in 60min window = 0 new alert row). (5) Admin-only gates: bob → 403 on both /overview and /alerts. (6) Regression OK: /api/wallet/send still works after rate-limit window expires. Frontend tab 'admin-tab-wallet-overview' mounts WalletOverviewAdminTab with all expected data-testids (wallet-overview-admin-tab, wallet-admin-window, wallet-admin-refresh, 4 wallet-kpi-*, wallet-alerts-feed, wallet-anomalies-banner), 2 Recharts ResponsiveContainers (LineChart inflow/outflow + BarChart volumes_by_type), Top 10 funded table populated (10 rows), and the 'SPAM SEND (DERNIÈRE HEURE)' banner correctly surfaces Bob's 20 sends (20 XAF). No functional console errors on this tab.

minor.issue: Intermittent 45s read-timeouts persist under load (carry-over from iter88). Retry logic in test fixture masks it but p99 login latency is concerning. Consider bcrypt round tuning or login-path caching.

action_items: None blocking. Wallet monitoring phase can be closed.

(Optional) The 27 console 401/403 errors observed during /admin page load come from OTHER admin tabs pre-fetching data (not from WalletOverviewAdminTab). Worth auditing which tabs pre-fetch before their own activation to reduce noise — not a regression.
(Carry-over from iter88) EngagementPointsCard.jsx L39 still navigates to non-existent /games route; not retested this round but remains an open MEDIUM-priority bug.
critical_code_review_comments: middleware/rate_limit.py: key_func _user_or_ip reads JWT UNVERIFIED (verify_signature=False). Acceptable for rate-limit bucketing (no integrity needed, it's just a stable handle), but worth commenting 'DO NOT use payload elsewhere'. Current code comment already flags this — good.

routes/wallet.py L322-323: @_limiter.limit('5/minute') is tight for a fintech send endpoint. Consider exposing this as an admin setting (rate_limit_send_per_minute) so ops can tune without redeploy. Non-blocking.

routes/wallet.py L340-342: spam_count+1 >= SEND_SPAM_COUNT triggers alert BEFORE the tx commits. If tx rolls back (e.g. insufficient balance on 6th attempt), the alert is already fired. Minor — alerting on attempted spam is arguably correct.

services/admin_alerts.py dedup window for withdraw_no_kyc=60min is reasonable but NOT configurable; a spammer could retry 25 times/hour before hitting an alert row. Consider adding an override setting.

WalletOverviewAdminTab.jsx L38-41: Promise.all without settled-fallback → if /alerts 500s, the entire tab stays on loading=true then shows toast 'Chargement impossible' and data=null. Consider Promise.allSettled to still render overview when alerts fails.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all testids, charts, tables render; no functional errors)

test_credentials: admin@japap.com / JapapAdmin2024! ; bob@japap.com / Test1234! ; alice@japap.com / Test1234!
seed_data_creation: None this round. Bob's wallet drained (~0 XAF from iter88) does NOT block rate-limit testing (slowapi runs before the handler so HTTP 400 insufficient-balance responses still consume the bucket and the 6th attempt returns 429 as required).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://japap-refactor.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iter89_wallet_monitoring.py -v -p no:ethereum (~100s). For the rate-limit test, no balance top-up needed (tests with amount=999999 force HTTP 400 before rate-limit window is exhausted — rate bucket still counts and 6th call = 429). Known: /api/auth/login occasionally 45s-timeouts → test retries 3x.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Wallet monitoring phase is feature-complete and production-ready.
